questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Positive number $x, y$ and $z$ satisfy $x y z=10^{81}$ and
$$
\left(\log _{10} x\right)\left(\log _{10} y z\right)+\left(\log _{10} y\right)\left(\log _{10} z\right)=468 \text {. Find } \sqrt{\left(\log _{10} x\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} y\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} z\right)^2} \text {. }
$$
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
(a+b+c)^2 & =a^2+b^2+c^2+2(a b+a c+b c) \\
\left(\log _{10} x\right)\left(\log _{10} y z\right) & =\left(\log _{10} x\right)\left(\log _{10} y+\log _{10} z\right) \\
& =\log _{10} x \log _{10} y+\log _{10} x \log _{10} z \\
\text { and } \quad x y z & =10^{81} \text { implies } \log _{10} x+\log _{10} y+\log _{10} z=81
\end{aligned}
$$
and
$$
x y z=10^{81} \text { implies } \log _{10} x+\log _{10} y+\log _{10} z=81
$$

Using the property mentioned first, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left(\log _{10} x\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} y\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} z\right)^2 \\
& =\left(\log _{10} x+\log _{10} y+\log _{10} z\right)^2-2\left(\log _{10} x \cdot \log _{10} y+\log _{10} x \cdot \log _{10} z+\log _{10} y \cdot \log _{10} z\right) \\
& \sqrt{\left(\log _{10} x\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} y\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} z\right)^2}=\sqrt{(81)^2-2 \times 468} \\
& \sqrt{\left(\log _{10} x\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} y\right)^2+\left(\log _{10} z\right)^2}=75
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $x=\left(\operatorname{antilog}_2 3\right) \cdot \log _3 2, y=\log _2\left(\log _3\left(\log _2 512\right)\right)$ and $z=\log _5 3 \cdot \log _7 5 \cdot \log _2 7$, then $x y z$ is equal to:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} x & =8 \log _3 2, \\ y & =\log _2\left(\log _3 9\right)=1, \\ z & =\log _5 3 \cdot \log _7 5 \cdot \log _2 7=\log _2 3 \\ \Rightarrow \quad x y z & =8\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the inequality $1+\log _5\left(x^2+1\right) \geq \log _5\left(a x^2+4 x+a\right)$ holds $\forall x \in R$, then the range of $a$ is $(p, q]$, find $(p+q)$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{rlrl} & \log _5\left(5 x^2+5\right) & \geq \log _5\left(a x^2+4 x+a\right) \forall x \in R \\ & \therefore & 5 x^2+5 & \geq a x^2+4 x+a \forall x \\ & \therefore & (5-a) x^2-4 x+5-a & \geq 0 \forall x \in R \\ 5-a & >0\end{array}$ and $$ \begin{aligned} & D \leq 0 \\ ...(2) & a < 5 \end{aligned} $$ and $$ \begin{aligned} 16-4(5-a)^2 & \leq 0 \\ 4-(5-a)^2 & \leq 0 \\ (a-5)^2-4 & \geq 0 \\ (a-3)(a-7) & \geq 0 \end{aligned} $$
From Eqns. (1) and (2) $\Rightarrow a \in(2,3]$ ...(3) $$ \therefore \quad p+q=5 $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Find the number of integral values of $x$ satisfying the inequality
$$
\frac{\left(2^{\left.\frac{\pi}{\tan ^{-1} x}-4\right)(x-4)(x-10)}\right.}{x !-(x-1) !} < 0
$$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \quad x !-(x-1) !>0 \\ & \therefore \underbrace{\left(2^{\frac{\pi}{\tan ^{-1} x}}-4\right)}_{\substack{\text { Minimum }>0 \\ \text { when } x \rightarrow \infty}}(x-4)(x-10) < 0 \\ & \therefore \quad x \in(4,10) \\ & \therefore \quad 5 \text { integral values i.e., } 5,6,7,8,9\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x_1$ and $x_2$ are the solutions of the equation $5^{\left(\log _5 x\right)^2}+x^{\log _5 x}=1250$, then $x_1 \cdot x_2$ is equal to:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} 5^{\left(\log _5 x\right)^2} & =x^{\log _5 x}=a \\ a+a & =1250 \Rightarrow a=625=x^{\log _5 x} \\ \left(\log _5 x\right)^2 & =\log _5 625=4 \\ \log _5 x & =2 \text { or }-2 \Rightarrow x=-25 \text { or } \frac{1}{25}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Find the number of integral values of $x$ satisfying the equation:
$$
\left|\log _2^2 x-5 \log _2 x+6\right|=\left|\log _2^2 x-7 \log _2 x+12\right|-\left|2 \log _2 x-6\right|
$$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \left|\log _2^2 x-5 \log _2 x+6\right|+\left|2 \log _2 x-6\right|=\left|\log _2^2 x-7 \log _2 x+12\right| \\ & |x|+|y|=|x-y| \Rightarrow x y \leq 0 \\ & \therefore\left(\log _2^2 x-5 \log _2 x+6\right)\left(2 \log _2 x-6\right) \leq 0 \\ & \left(\log _2 x-2\right)\left(\log _2 x-3\right)\left(\log _2 x-3\right) \leq 0 \\ & \left(\log _2 x-2\right)\left(\log _2 x-3\right)^2 \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \log _2 x \leq 2 \text { or } \log _2 x=3 \\ & \quad x \in(0,4] \cup\{8\}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $9^{1+\log x}=3^{1+\log x}+210$ (where base of $\log$ is 3 ) then integral value of $x$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} 3^{2+2 \log _3 x} & =3^{1+\log _3 x}+210 \\ 9 x^2 & =3 x+210 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 9 x^2-3 x-210 & =0 \Rightarrow x=5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
